Off Calendar Sep-12-2024 Off Calendar

Off Calendar Apr-11-2024 Set For Trial On Jan-13-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Apr-24-2024 Off Calendar

DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-29-2024 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. ALEXANDER SCHWARTZ, Esq. of Hess, Bower, Adams-Hess, PC (Tel: 408-341-0234) for Defendant/ Cross-complainant.
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: CROSS DEFENDANT CSE SAFEGUARD INSURANCE COMPANY's DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT.

Matter not argued/contested. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

CSE Safeguard Insurance Co.'s demurrer to Sally Kwok's cross-complaint (CC) is overruled.

Landlord Kwok was sued for habitability issues. CSE defended Kwok under a reservation of rights and paid a $800,000 settlement. CSE then sued Kwok for reimbursement of defense costs and the $800,000 indemnity. Kwok cross-claimed for breach of contract, breach of covenant of good faith and fair dealing and financial elder abuse.

CSE's primary argument on demurrer is that the CC focuses on "the filing of this lawsuit" as CSE's misconduct, though such reimbursement suits are allowed by Blue Ridge Ins. Co. v. Jacobsen (2001) 25 Cal.4th 489. (Memo. 3:9-12 see also 4:11-13, 5:13-15, 6:12-15, 7:15-22, 9:1-3.) However, the CC actually pleads not "the filing of this suit," but a long list of CSE misconduct. (Id. at 9:13-10:10.) Blue Ridge does not bar such cross-complaints.

CSE secondarily argues that its reservation of rights letter did not trigger a right to independent counsel. Even were that so, it would not matter, for the CC pleads other grounds. (Elder v. Pacific Bell Telephone Co. (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 841, 856 n.14 ["general demurrer does not lie to a portion of a cause of action"].)

Finally, the CC adequately alleges damages at the pleading stage - e.g., payments to her independent counsel, emotional distress, etc. (Id. at 8:13-23, 10:11-13, 10:18-19, 12:19-22, 13:15-27.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)

MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-29-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. ALEXANDER SCHWARTZ, Esq. of Hess, Bower, Adams-Hess, PC (Tel: 408-341-0234) for Defendant/ Cross-complainant.
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: CROSS DEFENDANT CSE SAFEGUARD INSURANCE COMPANY's MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT.

Matter not argued/contested. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

CSE Safeguard Insurance Co.'s "motion to strike [MTS] punitive damages" from Sally Kwok's cross-complaint (CC) is denied. The MTS is largely a repeat of CSE's demurrer, so the rulings on the former are incorporated by reference. (MTS 4:3-22.) The CC adequately alleges a basis for punitive damages at the pleading stage. (Id. at 6:12-8:23, 9:8-10:10, 10:17-13:27.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
